children.label: B

children.label: C

children.label: D
children.text: to ensure accountability and link performance outcomes to investment decisions;

children.label: E
children.text: to ensure connectivity and access for residents of rural areas of the United States through strategic investments in National Highway System bridges and bridges on all public roads; and

inventory all highway bridges on public roads, on and off Federal-aid highways, including tribally owned and Federally owned bridges, that are bridges over waterways, other topographical barriers, other highways, and railroads;
inventory all tunnels on public roads, on and off Federal-aid highways, including tribally owned and Federally owned tunnels;

classify the bridges according to serviceability, safety, and essentiality for public use, including the potential impacts to emergency evacuation routes and to regional and national freight and passenger mobility if the serviceability of the bridge is restricted or diminished;
based on that classification, assign each a risk-based priority for systematic preventative maintenance, replacement, or rehabilitation;
determine the cost of replacing each bridge classified as in poor condition identified under this subsection with a comparable facility or the cost of rehabilitating the bridge; and
determine if the replacement or rehabilitation of bridges and tunnels should include measures to enable safe and unimpeded movement for terrestrial and aquatic species.

children.heading: .—Inspection report
children.label: 2 .—Inspection report
children.text: Not later than 2 years after the date of enactment of the MAP–21, each State and appropriate Federal agency shall report element level data to the Secretary, as each bridge is inspected pursuant to this section, for all highway bridges on the National Highway System.

children.heading: .—Guidance
children.label: 3 .—Guidance
children.text: The Secretary shall provide guidance to States and Federal agencies for implementation of this subsection, while respecting the existing inspection schedule of each State.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: 1 .—In general
children.text: Notwithstanding any other provision of law, any bridge that is owned and operated by an agency that does not have taxing powers and whose functions include operating a federally assisted public transit system subsidized by toll revenues shall be eligible for assistance under this title, but the amount of such assistance shall in no event exceed the cumulative amount which such agency has expended for capital and operating costs to subsidize such transit system.

children.heading: .—Insufficient assets
children.label: 2 .—Insufficient assets
children.text: Before authorizing an expenditure of funds under this subsection, the Secretary shall determine that the applicant agency has insufficient reserves, surpluses, and projected revenues (over and above those required for bridge and transit capital and operating costs) to fund the bridge project or activity eligible for assistance under this title.

children.heading: .—Crediting of non-federal funds
children.label: 3 .—Crediting of non-federal funds
children.text: Any non-Federal funds expended for the seismic retrofit of the bridge may be credited toward the non-Federal share required as a condition of receipt of any Federal funds for seismic retrofit of the bridge made available after the date of the expenditure.

children.heading: .—Federal share
children.label: 2 .—Federal share
children.text: The Federal share payable on any bridge construction carried out under paragraph (1) shall be 80 percent of the cost of the construction.

children.heading: .—Definition of historic bridge
children.label: 1 .—Definition of historic bridge
children.text: In this subsection, the term “historic bridge” means any bridge that is listed on, or eligible for listing on, the National Register of Historic Places.

children.heading: .—Coordination
children.label: 2 .—Coordination
children.text: The Secretary shall, in cooperation with the States, encourage the retention, rehabilitation, adaptive reuse, and future study of historic bridges.

children.heading: .—State inventory
children.label: 3 .—State inventory
children.text: The Secretary shall require each State to complete an inventory of all bridges on and off Federal-aid highways to determine the historic significance of the bridges.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: A .—In general
children.text: Subject to subparagraph (B), reasonable costs associated with actions to preserve, or reduce the impact of a project under this chapter on, the historic integrity of a historic bridge shall be eligible as reimbursable project costs under section 133 if the load capacity and safety features of the historic bridge are adequate to serve the intended use for the life of the historic bridge.

children.heading: .—Bridges not used for vehicle traffic
children.label: B .—Bridges not used for vehicle traffic
children.text: In the case of a historic bridge that is no longer used for motorized vehicular traffic, the costs eligible as reimbursable project costs pursuant to this chapter shall not exceed the estimated cost of demolition of the historic bridge.

children.label: A

children.label: B
children.text: to assume all future legal and financial responsibility for the historic bridge, which may include an agreement to hold the State transportation department harmless in any liability action.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: A .—In general
children.text: Costs incurred by the State to preserve a historic bridge (including funds made available to the State, locality, or private entity to enable it to accept the bridge) shall be eligible as reimbursable project costs under this chapter in an amount not to exceed the cost of demolition.

children.heading: .—Additional funding
children.label: B .—Additional funding
children.text: Any bridge preserved pursuant to this paragraph shall not be eligible for any other funds authorized pursuant to this title.

children.heading: National Bridge and Tunnel Inspection Standards.—
children.label: h National Bridge and Tunnel Inspection Standards.—

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: A .—In general
children.text: The Secretary shall establish and maintain inspection standards for the proper inspection and evaluation of all highway bridges and tunnels for safety and serviceability.

children.heading: .—Uniformity
children.label: B .—Uniformity
children.text: The standards under this subsection shall be designed to ensure uniformity of the inspections and evaluations.

children.label: A
children.text: specify, in detail, the method by which the inspections shall be carried out by the States, Federal agencies, and tribal governments;

children.label: B
children.text: establish the maximum time period between inspections;

children.label: C
children.text: establish the qualifications for those charged with carrying out the inspections;

children.label: i
children.text: written reports on the results of highway bridge and tunnel inspections and notations of any action taken pursuant to the findings of the inspections; and

children.label: ii
children.text: current inventory data for all highway bridges and tunnels reflecting the findings of the most recent highway bridge and tunnel inspections conducted; and

children.label: E

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: A .—In general
children.text: The Secretary shall annually review State compliance with the standards established under this section.

children.label: i

children.label: I

children.label: II

children.label: 7 .—Risk-based approach
children.text: In carrying out the revisions required by paragraph (6), the Secretary shall consider a risk-based approach to determining the frequency of bridge inspections.

children.heading: Training Program for Bridge and Tunnel Inspectors.—
children.label: i Training Program for Bridge and Tunnel Inspectors.—

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: 1 .—In general
children.text: The Secretary, in cooperation with the State transportation departments, shall maintain a program designed to train appropriate personnel to carry out highway bridge and tunnel inspections.

children.heading: .—Revisions
children.label: 2 .—Revisions
children.text: The training program shall be revised from time to time to take into account new and improved techniques.

children.heading: .—Requirement
children.label: 3 .—Requirement
children.text: The first revision under paragraph (2) after the date of enactment of the Surface Transportation Reauthorization Act of 2021 shall include techniques to assess passage of aquatic and terrestrial species and habitat restoration potential.

children.heading: .—Purpose
children.label: 1 .—Purpose
children.text: The purpose of this subsection is to save costs and time by encouraging States to bundle multiple bridge projects as 1 project.
